message:
feat(notifications): tick 198 handle_detection_with_cx mute-check routes through is_event_muted_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx was mostly cx-threaded
— the outer entry checks mute_store via `storage.read_with_cx(cx)`
for the RwLock acquire, and the dispatch path uses
`dispatch_payload_with_cx(cx, ...)`. But the inner mute-check call:

    storage_guard.is_event_muted(&identity_key, now_ms).await

used the legacy ambient-cx method, not is_event_muted_with_cx(cx, ...).
storage.rs has had the cx-first sibling since tick 149 (L6532).

Under a caller-cancelled cx, the mute-check would still run to
completion at the DB layer even though the caller has abandoned
the notify flow. Net effect: wasted DB round-trip for a muted
check whose result the caller will discard.

Tick 198 routes through `is_event_muted_with_cx(cx, ...)`. Now the
mute-check honors the caller's explicit cx at the storage
pre-flight checkpoint.

Legacy `handle_detection` and its inner `is_event_muted` call are
unchanged.

Verification: 48 / 48 notifications:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass, including
`pipeline_with_cx_mute_store_blocks_muted_events` which pins the
mute-check contract for the cx-first variant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
